title: Push-Triggered Scheduler Strategy
description: Enable push-triggered implementation execution via pre-push git hook that detects approved specs and spawns sl implement as a detached background process delegating to Claude CLI.

title: Setup Phase: Package structure and command registration
description: WHY: New packages (scheduler/, spec/) and command registrations are prerequisites for all feature work.
design: Create pkg/cli/scheduler/ directory. Create pkg/cli/spec/ directory if not exists. Register VarApproveCmd, VarHookCmd, VarImplementCmd, VarLockCmd in cmd/sl/main.go. Follow existing Cobra VarXxxCmd pattern from config.go.

title: Create scheduler and spec package structure
description: WHY: New packages are needed for lock management, spec detection, and Claude CLI execution.

items.item: lock.go, detector.go, executor.go stub files created

design: Create pkg/cli/scheduler/ with empty lock.go, detector.go, executor.go. Create pkg/cli/spec/status.go if not exists. Each file starts with package declaration and doc comment. Follow existing package patterns (e.g., pkg/cli/hooks/).
acceptance_criteria: pkg/cli/scheduler/ exists with lock.go, detector.go, executor.go stubs. pkg/cli/spec/status.go exists. go build ./... succeeds.

title: Register new command stubs in cmd/sl/main.go
description: WHY: All new commands need Cobra registration to be accessible via the sl CLI.
design: In cmd/sl/main.go, add rootCmd.AddCommand() calls for VarApproveCmd, VarHookCmd, VarImplementCmd, VarLockCmd. Create minimal stub files in pkg/cli/commands/: approve.go, hook.go, implement.go, lock.go with empty VarXxxCmd vars. Follow the pattern in config.go.
acceptance_criteria: sl approve --help, sl hook --help, sl implement --help, sl lock --help all show usage. go build ./... succeeds.

title: Foundational: Spec status parsing and execution lock
description: WHY: Spec status parsing and execution lock management are shared prerequisites needed by the approve command (US0), hook detection (US1), and implement command (US1).
design: Implement spec status read/write in pkg/cli/spec/status.go. Implement ExecutionLock create/check/remove in pkg/cli/scheduler/lock.go. Follow data-model.md entity definitions.

title: Implement spec status read/write helpers
description: WHY: Multiple commands need to read and update the Status field in spec.md (approve sets it, hook execute checks it).

items.item: Handles missing spec.md with clear error

design: File: pkg/cli/spec/status.go. Functions: ReadStatus(specDir string) (string, error) - reads spec.md, finds line matching '**Status**: <value>', returns value. WriteStatus(specDir string, newStatus string) error - replaces status value in-place. Use line-by-line scan with strings.Contains for pattern matching. See research.md section 7.
acceptance_criteria: ReadStatus correctly parses Draft and Approved from spec.md. WriteStatus replaces Draft with Approved. Both handle missing file and malformed content gracefully.

title: Implement ExecutionLock management
description: WHY: Prevents duplicate sl implement runs. Lock is acquired before Claude CLI spawn and released after completion.

items.item: AcquireLock creates lock file with PID and timestamp
CheckLock reads and parses lock file
AcquireLock returns error if lock already held
Unit tests for all lock operations

design: File: pkg/cli/scheduler/lock.go. Struct ExecutionLock{PID int, Feature string, StartedAt string}. Functions: AcquireLock(feature string) error - write JSON to .specledger/exec.lock with current PID. CheckLock() (*ExecutionLock, error) - read and parse lock file, return nil if not exists. ReleaseLock() error - remove lock file. IsLockHeld() bool. See data-model.md ExecutionLock entity for field definitions.
acceptance_criteria: AcquireLock creates valid JSON lock file with PID, feature, ISO 8601 timestamp. CheckLock reads existing lock. ReleaseLock removes file. AcquireLock fails if lock already exists.

title: Implement approved spec detection logic
description: WHY: The pre-push hook needs to detect whether the current branch has an approved spec before triggering implementation.
design: File: pkg/cli/scheduler/detector.go. Functions: DetectApprovedSpec(projectRoot string) (*DetectionResult, error). Logic: 1) Get current branch name via go-git. 2) Check branch matches NNN-feature-name pattern (regex). 3) Resolve specledger/<branch>/spec.md path. 4) Use spec.ReadStatus to check if Approved. Return DetectionResult{Feature, SpecDir, Approved bool}. See FR-004, FR-009.
acceptance_criteria: Detects approved spec on feature branch. Returns nil for non-feature branches. Returns not-approved for Draft specs. Handles missing spec directory gracefully.

title: US0: Spec Approval Command
description: WHY: Without an approval mechanism, there is no trigger signal for the push hook. This is the prerequisite gate for automation.
design: Implement sl approve command per contracts/sl-hook-cli.md. Uses spec.ReadStatus/WriteStatus and artifact validation. See plan.md Phase 1.

title: Implement sl approve command
description: WHY: Users need a way to mark specs as approved, gating push-triggered implementation.

items.item: Status updated from Draft to Approved

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/approve.go. VarApproveCmd with --spec flag (default: auto-detect from branch). Behavior per contracts/sl-hook-cli.md: 1) Resolve spec context. 2) Validate spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md exist and non-empty. 3) Read status via spec.ReadStatus. 4) If Draft, write Approved via spec.WriteStatus. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=missing artifacts, 2=spec not found. Use RunE pattern.
acceptance_criteria: sl approve updates Status from Draft to Approved when all artifacts present. sl approve fails with clear message listing missing artifacts. sl approve reports already approved for approved specs. sl approve auto-detects spec from branch name.

title: US2: Hook Installation and Management
description: WHY: Users need a reliable way to install, uninstall, and check the status of the pre-push hook. Without this, the feature is not accessible.
design: Implement git hook management in pkg/cli/hooks/githook.go and sl hook commands in pkg/cli/commands/hook.go. Marker-based install/uninstall preserving existing hooks. See plan.md Phase 2 and contracts/sl-hook-cli.md.

title: Implement git hook install/uninstall/status logic
description: WHY: Core library for managing the pre-push hook file with marker-based blocks that preserve existing hooks.

items.item: Existing hook content preserved on install

design: File: pkg/cli/hooks/githook.go. Functions: InstallPushHook(gitDir string, force bool) error - read .git/hooks/pre-push, append/replace SPECLEDGER marker block, chmod +x. UninstallPushHook(gitDir string) error - remove marker block, delete file if empty. HasPushHook(gitDir string) bool - check for marker. Hook template from contracts/sl-hook-cli.md: BEGIN/END SPECLEDGER PUSH HOOK markers wrapping 'sl hook execute --event pre-push'. Follow pattern in pkg/cli/hooks/claude.go.
acceptance_criteria: Install creates hook file with marker block and shebang. Install preserves existing hook content. Uninstall removes only the marker block. Status correctly detects installation. chmod +x applied to hook file.

title: Implement sl hook install/uninstall/status commands
description: WHY: Users need CLI commands to manage the push hook lifecycle.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/hook.go. VarHookCmd as parent with subcommands: hookInstallCmd (--force flag, calls hooks.InstallPushHook), hookUninstallCmd (calls hooks.UninstallPushHook), hookStatusCmd (--json flag, calls hooks.HasPushHook). Follow contracts/sl-hook-cli.md for exit codes and output format. Use RunE pattern.
acceptance_criteria: sl hook install creates pre-push hook. sl hook install --force overwrites existing block. sl hook uninstall removes hook. sl hook status shows installed/not installed. sl hook status --json outputs JSON.

title: US1: Push-Triggered Implementation Execution
description: WHY: This is the core value proposition - automating the transition from planning to implementation via git push. Without this, the feature has no purpose.
design: Implement sl hook execute (internal), sl implement (Claude CLI wrapper), sl lock reset/status, and Claude CLI executor. See plan.md Phase 3 and contracts/sl-hook-cli.md.

title: Implement Claude CLI process spawning executor
description: WHY: The executor is the bridge between sl implement and the Claude CLI that performs actual task execution.
design: File: pkg/cli/scheduler/executor.go. Function: SpawnClaudeCLI(feature, projectRoot, logDir string) (*os.Process, error). Logic: 1) exec.LookPath('claude') to verify availability. 2) Create log file at logDir/<feature>-claude.log. 3) Build exec.Command('claude', '-p', '/specledger.implement', '--dangerously-skip-permissions'). 4) Set SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} for process group detach. 5) Set Dir to projectRoot. 6) Redirect Stdout/Stderr to log file. 7) cmd.Start(). Return process. See research.md section 5.
acceptance_criteria: Spawns claude process with correct args. Process detached via Setpgid. Output redirected to log file. Returns error if claude not in PATH. Works on macOS and Linux.

title: Implement sl implement command (Claude CLI wrapper)
description: WHY: This is the thin Go wrapper that manages the implementation lifecycle: lock, spawn Claude CLI, wait, cleanup.

items.item: Lock acquisition before Claude CLI spawn
Lock released via defer on completion
Error handling: claude not found, lock held
Exit code passthrough from Claude CLI

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/implement.go. VarImplementCmd with --feature flag. Behavior per contracts/sl-hook-cli.md: 1) Resolve spec context. 2) Verify claude in PATH. 3) Verify .claude/commands/specledger.implement.md exists. 4) AcquireLock(feature). 5) SpawnClaudeCLI(feature, root, logDir). 6) cmd.Wait() for completion. 7) ReleaseLock(). 8) Write result summary. Exit with claude exit code. Use defer for lock cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: sl implement acquires lock before spawning claude. sl implement waits for claude to complete. Lock released on success and failure. Result summary written. Error if claude not found. Error if lock already held.

title: Implement sl hook execute subcommand (internal)
description: WHY: The pre-push hook script calls this command to detect approved specs and spawn sl implement. This is the glue between git hooks and the implementation pipeline.

items.item: Detached background spawn of sl implement

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/hook.go (add hookExecuteCmd subcommand). Behavior per contracts/sl-hook-cli.md: 1) Get current branch. 2) Call detector.DetectApprovedSpec. 3) If not approved: log and exit 0. 4) Check lock via scheduler.CheckLock - if held: log and exit 0. 5) Spawn 'sl implement --feature <spec>' as detached background process using os/exec with Setpgid. 6) Log to push-hook.log. 7) Always exit 0. Never block push.
acceptance_criteria: Detects approved spec on feature branch and spawns sl implement. Skips non-feature branches silently. Skips unapproved specs silently. Skips if lock is held. Always exits 0 regardless of errors. Logs all actions to push-hook.log.

title: Implement sl lock reset and sl lock status commands
description: WHY: Users need manual recovery when a lock is left behind after a crash, and visibility into current lock state.

items.item: Graceful handling when no lock exists

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/lock.go. VarLockCmd with subcommands: lockResetCmd - calls scheduler.ReleaseLock(), prints confirmation. lockStatusCmd (--json flag) - calls scheduler.CheckLock(), prints lock info or 'no active lock'. See contracts/sl-hook-cli.md for output format.
acceptance_criteria: sl lock reset removes exec.lock unconditionally. sl lock reset reports no lock found if none exists. sl lock status shows PID, feature, started_at. sl lock status shows no lock message when none exists.

title: US3: Execution Feedback and Logging
description: WHY: Observability is important for trust and debugging. Developers need to see what the hook detected and whether implementation was triggered.
design: Implement structured log writing to .specledger/logs/push-hook.log with rotation (50 entries). Add error display to sl hook status. See plan.md Phase 4 and data-model.md HookExecutionLog entity.

title: Implement structured hook execution log writer
description: WHY: Hook execution details must be logged for debugging and auditability.

items.item: Unit tests for log writing and rotation

design: File: pkg/cli/scheduler/log.go (new). Function: WriteHookLog(logDir, branch, feature, action, detail string) error. Format: [timestamp] branch=X feature=X action=X detail=X (one line per entry, append-only). Create .specledger/logs/ directory if not exists. Implement log rotation: keep last 50 entries per SC-005. See data-model.md HookExecutionLog entity.
acceptance_criteria: Log entries written in structured format with timestamp. Log file created in .specledger/logs/push-hook.log. Log rotation keeps last 50 entries. Directory auto-created if missing.

title: Add recent error display to sl hook status
description: WHY: Developers need quick visibility into recent hook errors without manually reading log files.

items.item: sl hook status displays last 5 errors

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/hook.go (extend hookStatusCmd). Add ReadRecentErrors(logDir string, count int) ([]LogEntry, error) to read last N error entries from push-hook.log. Display last 5 errors below hook status output. See plan.md Phase 3 error handling.
acceptance_criteria: sl hook status shows last 5 errors from push-hook.log. Shows nothing extra if no errors. Works with empty or missing log file.

title: Polish: Integration tests and cross-platform verification
description: WHY: End-to-end verification ensures the full workflow (approve → push → hook → implement) works correctly.
design: Integration tests in tests/ directory. Test full approve workflow, hook install lifecycle, and hook execute flow with mock specs. Verify cross-platform (macOS/Linux) compatibility of hook scripts and process detachment.

title: Integration test: approve command workflow
description: WHY: Verify the full approval workflow with real file system operations.
design: File: tests/approve_integration_test.go. Test cases: 1) Approve with all artifacts present → status changes. 2) Approve with missing artifacts → error with list. 3) Approve already-approved → idempotent. 4) Auto-detect spec from branch name. Use t.TempDir() for isolated test environments.
acceptance_criteria: All approve acceptance scenarios from spec.md US0 verified. Tests pass on both macOS and Linux.

title: Integration test: hook install/uninstall lifecycle
description: WHY: Verify hook management works with real git repositories and existing hooks.
design: File: tests/hook_install_test.go. Test cases: 1) Install on clean repo. 2) Install preserving existing hooks. 3) Uninstall leaves other hooks. 4) Status detection. 5) Force reinstall. Use git init in t.TempDir() for isolated repos.
acceptance_criteria: All hook management acceptance scenarios from spec.md US2 verified. Existing hooks preserved correctly.
